---
title: "MusicRecs-Pipeline"
output: html_notebook
---

### Step 1: Load Libraries and Raw Dataset
```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(cluster)     
library(factoextra)  

# DATASET SOURCE:
# This dataset originates from the Kaggle "Spotify Tracks Dataset" by Maharshi Pandya.
# URL: [https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/maharshipandya/-spotify-tracks-dataset](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/maharshipandya/-spotify-tracks-dataset)
# It contains ~114,000 tracks pulled via the Spotify Web API.

# Load the dataset (Make sure dataset.csv is in your working directory)
songs <- read_csv("dataset.csv")
```

```{r}
merged_songs <- songs %>%
  group_by(track_id) %>%
  summarise(
    artists = first(artists),
    album_name = first(album_name),
    track_name = first(track_name),
    popularity = max(popularity),      
    duration_ms = first(duration_ms),
    explicit = any(explicit == "True"), 
    danceability = first(danceability),
    energy = first(energy),
    key = first(key),
    loudness = first(loudness),
    mode = first(mode),
    speechiness = first(speechiness),
    acousticness = first(acousticness),
    instrumentalness = first(instrumentalness),
    liveness = first(liveness),
    valence = first(valence),
    tempo = first(tempo),
    time_signature = first(time_signature),
    track_genre = paste(unique(track_genre), collapse = ", ")
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

merged_songs <- na.omit(merged_songs)
```

```{r}
# 1. Add a temporary ID so we can match the dummies back to the original rows
merged_songs <- merged_songs %>% mutate(row_id = row_number())

# 2. Create the dummy variables
genre_dummies <- merged_songs %>%
  select(row_id, track_genre) %>%
  separate_rows(track_genre, sep = ",\\s*") %>% 
  mutate(value = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = track_genre, 
              values_from = value, 
              values_fill = 0, 
              names_prefix = "genre_")

# 3. Join back to the original dataframe and remove the temporary ID
merged_songs <- merged_songs %>%
  left_join(genre_dummies, by = "row_id") %>%
  select(-row_id)
```

```{r}
# 1. Create a numeric-only dataframe for the algorithm
numeric_data <- merged_songs %>% 
  select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  scale()

# 2. Safety Check: Ensure the requested centers do not exceed our distinct row count
n_distinct_rows <- nrow(unique(numeric_data))
target_centers <- min(11500, n_distinct_rows - 1)

# 3. Run the model
set.seed(333)
kmeans_model <- kmeans(numeric_data, centers = target_centers)

# 4. Add the cluster assignments back to your dataframe
merged_songs$cluster <- kmeans_model$cluster
```

```{r}
# 1. Calculate distances and attach to individual rows
centers_matrix <- kmeans_model$centers[kmeans_model$cluster, ]
distances <- sqrt(rowSums((numeric_data - centers_matrix)^2))
merged_songs$dist_to_center <- distances

# 2. Create the final clean combination dataframe
cleaned_songs <- merged_songs %>%
  group_by(track_name, artists) %>%
  summarise(
    cluster = first(cluster),
    track_id = first(track_id),
    explicit = any(explicit == TRUE),
    popularity = max(popularity),
    album_name = first(album_name),
    track_genre = first(track_genre),
    across(where(is.numeric) & !c(cluster, popularity), mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# This is the file our Shiny notebook reads!
write_csv(cleaned_songs, "Unified_Music_Analysis_Final.csv")
print("SUCCESS: Unified_Music_Analysis_Final.csv has been created!")
```
